You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 7f323bef).

## Issue to Resolve

`ij_kotlin_imports_layout` with `|` generates multiple line breaks
## Expected Behavior
I'm currently using the `import-ordering` rule with the following config:
```
ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *, |, javax.**, java.**, |, kotlinx.**, kotlin.**
```

I'd expect one of my files to be formatted like:
```
package test

import java.nio.file.Files
import java.nio.file.Paths
import java.util.Objects

object TestData {
    fun loadString(dataset: String): String {
        val path = Paths.get(Objects.requireNonNull(TestData::class.java.getResource(dataset)).toURI())
        return String(Files.readAllBytes(path))
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
For some reason (I assume because of the two `|` in the layout config and not having any imports that match `*`), I got two extra line breaks in the file:
```
package test



import java.nio.file.Files
import java.nio.file.Paths
import java.util.Objects

object TestData {
    fun loadString(dataset: String): String {
        val path = Paths.get(Objects.requireNonNull(TestData::class.java.getResource(dataset)).toURI())
        return String(Files.readAllBytes(path))
    }
}
```

It seems that the number of extra line breaks is equals the number of `|` in the config, but if I remove the extra line breaks, I have to run `ktlint -F` twice until it stabilises, which makes the issue even worse.

## Steps to Reproduce
Use the configuration and file above.

## Your Environment
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.2
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings: (mentioned above)

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This seems to be a regression bug in `0.48.2`. It works fine with `0.48.1`.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ImportOrderingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.ImportOrderingRule.Companion.ASCII_PATTERN
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.ImportOrderingRule.Companion.IDEA_PATTERN
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.ImportSorter
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.parseImportsLayout
 14→import mu.KotlinLogging
 15→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 20→
 21→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * Import ordering is configured via EditorConfig's property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`, so the Kotlin IDE plugin also recongizes it. Supported values:
 25→ * * "*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^" - default IntelliJ IDEA's order, see [IDEA_PATTERN]
 26→ * * "*" - alphabetical order as recommended in Android's Kotlin style guide, see [ASCII_PATTERN]
 27→ * * custom - defined by the following set of tokens. Tokens can be combined together in a group, groups/tokens must be comma separated:
 28→ *  * "*" - wildcard symbol, can be used as follows:
 29→ *      1. Single, meaning matching any import (<all other imports> in IDEA)
 30→ *      2. After an import path, e.g. "java.*" or "kotlin.io.Closeable.*"
 31→ *      3. Doubled after an import path, e.g. "java.**" or "kotlin.io.**", meaning "with subpackages"
 32→ *  * "|" - blank line symbol. Only supported single blank lines between imports. Multiple blank lines will be ignored. Blank lines are not allowed outside of import list.
 33→ *  * "^" - alias symbol, can be used as follows:
 34→ *      1. In front of an import path, meaning matching all alias imports from this path, e.g. "^android.*"
 35→ *      2. Alone, meaning matching any alias import - "^" (<all other alias imports> in IDEA)
 36→ *  * import paths - these can be full paths, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard paths meaning "with subpackages", e.g. "kotlin.**"
 37→ *
 38→ * In case the custom property is not provided, the rule defaults to alphabetical order in case of "android" flag supplied, or to idea otherwise.
 39→ */
 40→public class ImportOrderingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "import-ordering",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY),
 44→    ) {
 45→    private lateinit var importsLayout: List<PatternEntry>
 46→    private lateinit var importSorter: ImportSorter
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        importsLayout = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY]
 50→        importSorter = ImportSorter(importsLayout)
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 54→        node: ASTNode,
 55→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57→    ) {
 58→        if (node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_LIST) {
 59→            val children = node.getChildren(null)
 60→            if (children.isNotEmpty()) {
 61→                // Get unique imports and blank lines
 62→                val (autoCorrectDuplicateImports: Boolean, imports: List<ASTNode>) =
 63→                    getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(children, emit)
 64→
 65→                val hasComments = children.any { it.elementType == ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT || it.elementType == ElementType.EOL_COMMENT }
 66→                val sortedImports = imports
 67→                    .asSequence()
 68→                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtImportDirective } // sorter expects KtImportDirective, whitespaces are inserted afterwards
 69→                    .sortedWith(importSorter)
 70→                    .map { it.node } // transform back to ASTNode in order to operate over its method (addChild)
 71→
 72→                // insert blank lines wherever needed
 73→                // traverse the list using fold to have previous and current element and decide if the blank line is needed in between
 74→                // based on the ImportSorter imported patterns and indexes from the comparator
 75→                val sortedImportsWithSpaces = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
 76→                sortedImports.fold(null as ASTNode?) { prev, current ->
 77→                    val index1 = if (prev == null) -1 else importSorter.findImportIndex((prev.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 78→                    val index2 = importSorter.findImportIndex((current.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 79→
 80→                    var hasBlankLines = false
 81→                    for (i in (index1 + 1) until index2) {
 82→                        if (importSorter.patterns[i] == PatternEntry.BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) {
 83→                            hasBlankLines = true
 84→                            break
 85→                        }
 86→                    }
 87→                    if (hasBlankLines) {
 88→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces += PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n\n")
 89→                    }
 90→                    sortedImportsWithSpaces += current
 91→
 92→                    return@fold current
 93→                }
 94→
 95→                if (hasComments) {
 96→                    emit(
 97→                        node.startOffset,
 98→                        ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE) +
 99→                            " -- no autocorrection due to comments in the import list",
100→                        false,
101→                    )
102→                } else {
103→                    val autoCorrectWhitespace = hasTooMuchWhitespace(children) && !isCustomLayout()
104→                    val autoCorrectSortOrder = !importsAreEqual(imports, sortedImportsWithSpaces)
105→                    if (autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace) {
106→                        emit(
107→                            node.startOffset,
108→                            ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE),
109→                            true,
110→                        )
111→                    }
112→                    if (autoCorrect && (autoCorrectDuplicateImports || autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace)) {
113→                        node.removeRange(node.firstChildNode, node.lastChildNode.treeNext)
114→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces.reduce { current, next ->
115→                            node.addChild(current, null)
116→                            if (current !is PsiWhiteSpace && next !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
117→                                node.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n"), null)
118→                            }
119→                            return@reduce next
120→                        }
121→                        node.addChild(sortedImportsWithSpaces.last(), null)
122→                    }
123→                }
124→            }
125→        }
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(
129→        children: Array<ASTNode>,
130→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
131→    ): Pair<Boolean, List<ASTNode>> {
132→        var autoCorrectDuplicateImports = false
133→        val imports = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
134→        val importTextSet = mutableSetOf<String>()
135→
136→        children.forEach { current ->
137→            val isPsiWhiteSpace = current.psi is PsiWhiteSpace
138→
139→            if (current.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE ||
140→                isPsiWhiteSpace && current.textLength > 1 // also collect empty lines, that are represented as "\n\n"
141→            ) {
142→                if (isPsiWhiteSpace || importTextSet.add(current.text)) {
143→                    imports += current
144→                } else {
145→                    emit(
146→                        current.startOffset,
147→                        "Duplicate '${current.text}' found",
148→                        true,
149→                    )
150→                    autoCorrectDuplicateImports = true
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        return autoCorrectDuplicateImports to imports
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun importsAreEqual(
159→        actual: List<ASTNode>,
160→        expected: List<ASTNode>,
161→    ): Boolean {
162→        if (actual.size != expected.size) return false
163→
164→        val combined = actual.zip(expected)
165→        return combined.all { (first, second) ->
166→            if (first is PsiWhiteSpace && second is PsiWhiteSpace) {
167→                return@all (first as PsiWhiteSpace).text == (second as PsiWhiteSpace).text
168→            }
169→            return@all first == second
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun isCustomLayout() = importsLayout != IDEA_PATTERN && importsLayout != ASCII_PATTERN
174→
175→    private fun hasTooMuchWhitespace(nodes: Array<ASTNode>): Boolean {
176→        return nodes.any { it is PsiWhiteSpace && (it as PsiWhiteSpace).text != "\n" }
177→    }
178→
179→    public companion object {
180→        /**
181→         * Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a).
182→         * No blank lines between major groups (android, com, junit, net, org, java, javax).
183→         * Single group regardless of import type.
184→         *
185→         * https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#import_statements
186→         */
187→        private val ASCII_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*")
188→
189→        /**
190→         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a),
191→         * except such groups as "java", "javax" and "kotlin" that are placed in the end. Within the groups the alphabetical order is preserved.
192→         * Alias imports are placed in a separate group in the end of the list with alphabetical order inside.
193→         * No blank lines between groups.
194→         *
195→         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L87-L91
196→         */
197→        private val IDEA_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^")
198→
199→        private const val IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between " +
200→            "with \"java\", \"javax\", \"kotlin\" and aliases in the end"
201→        private const val ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between"
202→        private const val CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered according to the pattern specified in .editorconfig"
203→
204→        private val ERROR_MESSAGES = mapOf(
205→            IDEA_PATTERN to IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE,
206→            ASCII_PATTERN to ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE,
207→        )
208→
209→        private val EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
210→            { _, value ->
211→                when {
212→                    value.isNullOrBlank() -> PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
213→                        value,
214→                        "Import layout must contain at least one entry of a wildcard symbol (*)",
215→                    )
216→                    value == "idea" -> {
217→                        LOGGER.warn {
218→                            "`idea` is deprecated! Please use `*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE " +
219→                                "plugin recognizes the value"
220→                        }
221→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
222→                            value,
223→                            IDEA_PATTERN,
224→                        )
225→                    }
226→                    value == "ascii" -> {
227→                        LOGGER.warn {
228→                            "`ascii` is deprecated! Please use `*` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE plugin recognizes the value"
229→                        }
230→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
231→                            value,
232→                            ASCII_PATTERN,
233→                        )
234→                    }
235→                    else -> try {
236→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
237→                            value,
238→                            parseImportsLayout(value),
239→                        )
240→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
241→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
242→                            value,
243→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
244→                        )
245→                    }
246→                }
247→            }
248→
249→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
250→            EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>>(
251→                type = PropertyType(
252→                    "ij_kotlin_imports_layout",
253→                    "Defines imports order layout for Kotlin files",
254→                    EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER,
255→                ),
256→                defaultValue = IDEA_PATTERN,
257→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = ASCII_PATTERN,
258→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
259→            )
260→    }
261→}
262→
263→public val IMPORT_ORDERING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ImportOrderingRule().ruleId
264→

0→# Changelog
  1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  3→
  4→## Unreleased
  5→
  6→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  7→
  8→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
  9→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 10→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 11→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 12→
 13→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 14→
 15→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 16→
 17→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 18→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 19→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 20→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 21→
 22→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 23→
 24→#### Experimental rules
 25→
 26→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 27→
 28→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 29→
 30→#### EditorConfig
 31→
 32→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 33→
 34→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 35→
 36→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 37→
 38→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 39→
 40→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 41→
 42→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 43→
 44→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 45→
 46→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 47→
 48→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 49→
 50→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 51→
 52→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 53→
 54→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 55→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 56→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 64→
 65→
 66→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 67→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 68→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |

150→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
151→
152→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
153→
154→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
155→
156→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
157→
158→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
159→
160→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
161→
162→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
163→
164→```kotlin
165→public class SomeRule : Rule(
166→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
167→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
168→) {
169→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
170→
171→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
172→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
173→  }
174→  
175→  ...
176→}
177→```
178→
179→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
180→
181→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
182→
183→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
184→
185→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
186→
187→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
188→
189→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
190→
191→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
192→
193→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
194→
195→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
196→
197→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.

CHANGELOG.md-278-Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.

CHANGELOG.md-280-The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:

CHANGELOG.md-282-[*.{kt,kts}]
CHANGELOG.md-283-ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *

CHANGELOG.md-285-ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
CHANGELOG.md-286-```
CHANGELOG.md-287-Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
CHANGELOG.md-288-
CHANGELOG.md-289-Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.

CHANGELOG.md-485-Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.

 26→ * * "*" - alphabetical order as recommended in Android's Kotlin style guide, see [ASCII_PATTERN]
 27→ * * custom - defined by the following set of tokens. Tokens can be combined together in a group, groups/tokens must be comma separated:
 28→ *  * "*" - wildcard symbol, can be used as follows:
 29→ *      1. Single, meaning matching any import (<all other imports> in IDEA)
 30→ *      2. After an import path, e.g. "java.*" or "kotlin.io.Closeable.*"
 31→ *      3. Doubled after an import path, e.g. "java.**" or "kotlin.io.**", meaning "with subpackages"
 32→ *  * "|" - blank line symbol. Only supported single blank lines between imports. Multiple blank lines will be ignored. Blank lines are not allowed outside of import list.

 35→ *      2. Alone, meaning matching any alias import - "^" (<all other alias imports> in IDEA)
 36→ *  * import paths - these can be full paths, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard paths meaning "with subpackages", e.g. "kotlin.**"
 37→ *

 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "import-ordering",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY),
 44→    ) {
 45→    private lateinit var importsLayout: List<PatternEntry>
 46→    private lateinit var importSorter: ImportSorter
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        importsLayout = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY]

 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 54→        node: ASTNode,
 55→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 56→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 57→    ) {
 58→        if (node.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_LIST) {
 59→            val children = node.getChildren(null)
 60→            if (children.isNotEmpty()) {
 61→                // Get unique imports and blank lines
 62→                val (autoCorrectDuplicateImports: Boolean, imports: List<ASTNode>) =
 63→                    getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(children, emit)
 64→
 65→                val hasComments = children.any { it.elementType == ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT || it.elementType == ElementType.EOL_COMMENT }
 66→                val sortedImports = imports
 67→                    .asSequence()
 68→                    .mapNotNull { it.psi as? KtImportDirective } // sorter expects KtImportDirective, whitespaces are inserted afterwards
 69→                    .sortedWith(importSorter)
 70→                    .map { it.node } // transform back to ASTNode in order to operate over its method (addChild)
 71→
 72→                // insert blank lines wherever needed
 73→                // traverse the list using fold to have previous and current element and decide if the blank line is needed in between
 74→                // based on the ImportSorter imported patterns and indexes from the comparator
 75→                val sortedImportsWithSpaces = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
 76→                sortedImports.fold(null as ASTNode?) { prev, current ->
 77→                    val index1 = if (prev == null) -1 else importSorter.findImportIndex((prev.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 78→                    val index2 = importSorter.findImportIndex((current.psi as KtImportDirective).importPath!!)
 79→
 80→                    var hasBlankLines = false
 81→                    for (i in (index1 + 1) until index2) {
 82→                        if (importSorter.patterns[i] == PatternEntry.BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) {
 83→                            hasBlankLines = true
 84→                            break
 85→                        }
 86→                    }
 87→                    if (hasBlankLines) {
 88→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces += PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n\n")
 89→                    }
 90→                    sortedImportsWithSpaces += current
 91→
 92→                    return@fold current
 93→                }
 94→
 95→                if (hasComments) {
 96→                    emit(
 97→                        node.startOffset,
 98→                        ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE) +
 99→                            " -- no autocorrection due to comments in the import list",
100→                        false,
101→                    )
102→                } else {
103→                    val autoCorrectWhitespace = hasTooMuchWhitespace(children) && !isCustomLayout()
104→                    val autoCorrectSortOrder = !importsAreEqual(imports, sortedImportsWithSpaces)
105→                    if (autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace) {
106→                        emit(
107→                            node.startOffset,
108→                            ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE),
109→                            true,
110→                        )
111→                    }
112→                    if (autoCorrect && (autoCorrectDuplicateImports || autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace)) {
113→                        node.removeRange(node.firstChildNode, node.lastChildNode.treeNext)
114→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces.reduce { current, next ->
115→                            node.addChild(current, null)
116→                            if (current !is PsiWhiteSpace && next !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
117→                                node.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n"), null)
118→                            }
119→                            return@reduce next
120→                        }
121→                        node.addChild(sortedImportsWithSpaces.last(), null)
122→                    }
123→                }
124→            }
125→        }
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(
129→        children: Array<ASTNode>,
130→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
131→    ): Pair<Boolean, List<ASTNode>> {
132→        var autoCorrectDuplicateImports = false
133→        val imports = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
134→        val importTextSet = mutableSetOf<String>()
135→
136→        children.forEach { current ->
137→            val isPsiWhiteSpace = current.psi is PsiWhiteSpace
138→
139→            if (current.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE ||
140→                isPsiWhiteSpace && current.textLength > 1 // also collect empty lines, that are represented as "\n\n"
141→            ) {
142→                if (isPsiWhiteSpace || importTextSet.add(current.text)) {
143→                    imports += current
144→                } else {
145→                    emit(
146→                        current.startOffset,
147→                        "Duplicate '${current.text}' found",
148→                        true,
149→                    )
150→                    autoCorrectDuplicateImports = true
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        return autoCorrectDuplicateImports to imports
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun importsAreEqual(
159→        actual: List<ASTNode>,
160→        expected: List<ASTNode>,
161→    ): Boolean {
162→        if (actual.size != expected.size) return false
163→
164→        val combined = actual.zip(expected)
165→        return combined.all { (first, second) ->
166→            if (first is PsiWhiteSpace && second is PsiWhiteSpace) {
167→                return@all (first as PsiWhiteSpace).text == (second as PsiWhiteSpace).text
168→            }
169→            return@all first == second
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun isCustomLayout() = importsLayout != IDEA_PATTERN && importsLayout != ASCII_PATTERN
174→
175→    private fun hasTooMuchWhitespace(nodes: Array<ASTNode>): Boolean {
176→        return nodes.any { it is PsiWhiteSpace && (it as PsiWhiteSpace).text != "\n" }
177→    }

179→    public companion object {
180→        /**
181→         * Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a).
182→         * No blank lines between major groups (android, com, junit, net, org, java, javax).
183→         * Single group regardless of import type.
184→         *
185→         * https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#import_statements
186→         */
187→        private val ASCII_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*")
188→
189→        /**
190→         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a),
191→         * except such groups as "java", "javax" and "kotlin" that are placed in the end. Within the groups the alphabetical order is preserved.
192→         * Alias imports are placed in a separate group in the end of the list with alphabetical order inside.
193→         * No blank lines between groups.
194→         *
195→         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L87-L91
196→         */
197→        private val IDEA_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^")
198→
199→        private const val IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between " +
200→            "with \"java\", \"javax\", \"kotlin\" and aliases in the end"
201→        private const val ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between"
202→        private const val CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered according to the pattern specified in .editorconfig"
203→
204→        private val ERROR_MESSAGES = mapOf(
205→            IDEA_PATTERN to IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE,
206→            ASCII_PATTERN to ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE,
207→        )
208→
209→        private val EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
210→            { _, value ->
211→                when {
212→                    value.isNullOrBlank() -> PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
213→                        value,
214→                        "Import layout must contain at least one entry of a wildcard symbol (*)",
215→                    )
216→                    value == "idea" -> {
217→                        LOGGER.warn {
218→                            "`idea` is deprecated! Please use `*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE " +
219→                                "plugin recognizes the value"
220→                        }
221→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
222→                            value,
223→                            IDEA_PATTERN,
224→                        )
225→                    }
226→                    value == "ascii" -> {
227→                        LOGGER.warn {
228→                            "`ascii` is deprecated! Please use `*` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE plugin recognizes the value"
229→                        }
230→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
231→                            value,
232→                            ASCII_PATTERN,
233→                        )
234→                    }
235→                    else -> try {
236→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
237→                            value,
238→                            parseImportsLayout(value),
239→                        )
240→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
241→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
242→                            value,
243→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
244→                        )
245→                    }
246→                }
247→            }
248→
249→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
250→            EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>>(
251→                type = PropertyType(
252→                    "ij_kotlin_imports_layout",
253→                    "Defines imports order layout for Kotlin files",
254→                    EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER,
255→                ),
256→                defaultValue = IDEA_PATTERN,
257→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = ASCII_PATTERN,
258→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
259→            )
260→    }
261→}

 96→                    emit(
 97→                        node.startOffset,
 98→                        ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE) +
 99→                            " -- no autocorrection due to comments in the import list",
100→                        false,
101→                    )
102→                } else {
103→                    val autoCorrectWhitespace = hasTooMuchWhitespace(children) && !isCustomLayout()
104→                    val autoCorrectSortOrder = !importsAreEqual(imports, sortedImportsWithSpaces)
105→                    if (autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace) {
106→                        emit(
107→                            node.startOffset,
108→                            ERROR_MESSAGES.getOrDefault(importsLayout, CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE),
109→                            true,
110→                        )
111→                    }
112→                    if (autoCorrect && (autoCorrectDuplicateImports || autoCorrectSortOrder || autoCorrectWhitespace)) {
113→                        node.removeRange(node.firstChildNode, node.lastChildNode.treeNext)
114→                        sortedImportsWithSpaces.reduce { current, next ->
115→                            node.addChild(current, null)
116→                            if (current !is PsiWhiteSpace && next !is PsiWhiteSpace) {
117→                                node.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl("\n"), null)
118→                            }
119→                            return@reduce next
120→                        }
121→                        node.addChild(sortedImportsWithSpaces.last(), null)
122→                    }
123→                }
124→            }
125→        }
126→    }
127→
128→    private fun getUniqueImportsAndBlankLines(
129→        children: Array<ASTNode>,
130→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
131→    ): Pair<Boolean, List<ASTNode>> {
132→        var autoCorrectDuplicateImports = false
133→        val imports = mutableListOf<ASTNode>()
134→        val importTextSet = mutableSetOf<String>()
135→
136→        children.forEach { current ->
137→            val isPsiWhiteSpace = current.psi is PsiWhiteSpace
138→
139→            if (current.elementType == ElementType.IMPORT_DIRECTIVE ||
140→                isPsiWhiteSpace && current.textLength > 1 // also collect empty lines, that are represented as "\n\n"
141→            ) {
142→                if (isPsiWhiteSpace || importTextSet.add(current.text)) {
143→                    imports += current
144→                } else {
145→                    emit(
146→                        current.startOffset,
147→                        "Duplicate '${current.text}' found",
148→                        true,
149→                    )
150→                    autoCorrectDuplicateImports = true
151→                }
152→            }
153→        }
154→
155→        return autoCorrectDuplicateImports to imports
156→    }
157→
158→    private fun importsAreEqual(
159→        actual: List<ASTNode>,
160→        expected: List<ASTNode>,
161→    ): Boolean {
162→        if (actual.size != expected.size) return false
163→
164→        val combined = actual.zip(expected)
165→        return combined.all { (first, second) ->
166→            if (first is PsiWhiteSpace && second is PsiWhiteSpace) {
167→                return@all (first as PsiWhiteSpace).text == (second as PsiWhiteSpace).text
168→            }
169→            return@all first == second
170→        }
171→    }
172→
173→    private fun isCustomLayout() = importsLayout != IDEA_PATTERN && importsLayout != ASCII_PATTERN
174→
175→    private fun hasTooMuchWhitespace(nodes: Array<ASTNode>): Boolean {
176→        return nodes.any { it is PsiWhiteSpace && (it as PsiWhiteSpace).text != "\n" }
177→    }

179→    public companion object {
180→        /**
181→         * Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a).
182→         * No blank lines between major groups (android, com, junit, net, org, java, javax).
183→         * Single group regardless of import type.
184→         *
185→         * https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#import_statements
186→         */
187→        private val ASCII_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*")
188→
189→        /**
190→         * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a),
191→         * except such groups as "java", "javax" and "kotlin" that are placed in the end. Within the groups the alphabetical order is preserved.
192→         * Alias imports are placed in a separate group in the end of the list with alphabetical order inside.
193→         * No blank lines between groups.
194→         *
195→         * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L87-L91
196→         */
197→        private val IDEA_PATTERN = parseImportsLayout("*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^")
198→
199→        private const val IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between " +
200→            "with \"java\", \"javax\", \"kotlin\" and aliases in the end"
201→        private const val ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered in lexicographic order without any empty lines in-between"
202→        private const val CUSTOM_ERROR_MESSAGE = "Imports must be ordered according to the pattern specified in .editorconfig"
203→
204→        private val ERROR_MESSAGES = mapOf(
205→            IDEA_PATTERN to IDEA_ERROR_MESSAGE,
206→            ASCII_PATTERN to ASCII_ERROR_MESSAGE,
207→        )
208→
209→        private val EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
210→            { _, value ->
211→                when {
212→                    value.isNullOrBlank() -> PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
213→                        value,
214→                        "Import layout must contain at least one entry of a wildcard symbol (*)",
215→                    )
216→                    value == "idea" -> {
217→                        LOGGER.warn {
218→                            "`idea` is deprecated! Please use `*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE " +
219→                                "plugin recognizes the value"
220→                        }
221→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
222→                            value,
223→                            IDEA_PATTERN,
224→                        )
225→                    }
226→                    value == "ascii" -> {
227→                        LOGGER.warn {
228→                            "`ascii` is deprecated! Please use `*` instead to ensure that the Kotlin IDE plugin recognizes the value"
229→                        }
230→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
231→                            value,
232→                            ASCII_PATTERN,
233→                        )
234→                    }
235→                    else -> try {
236→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
237→                            value,
238→                            parseImportsLayout(value),
239→                        )
240→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
241→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
242→                            value,
243→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
244→                        )
245→                    }
246→                }
247→            }
248→
249→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_IMPORTS_LAYOUT_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
250→            EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>>(
251→                type = PropertyType(
252→                    "ij_kotlin_imports_layout",
253→                    "Defines imports order layout for Kotlin files",
254→                    EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY_PARSER,
255→                ),
256→                defaultValue = IDEA_PATTERN,
257→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = ASCII_PATTERN,
258→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
259→            )
260→    }
261→}
262→
263→public val IMPORT_ORDERING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ImportOrderingRule().ruleId

0→# Changelog
  1→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  2→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  3→
  4→## Unreleased
  5→
  6→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  7→
  8→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
  9→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 10→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 11→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 12→
 13→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 14→
 15→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 16→
 17→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 18→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 19→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 20→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 21→
 22→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 23→
 24→#### Experimental rules
 25→
 26→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 27→
 28→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 29→
 30→#### EditorConfig
 31→
 32→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 33→
 34→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 35→
 36→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 37→
 38→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 39→
 40→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 41→
 42→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 43→
 44→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 45→
 46→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 47→
 48→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 49→
 50→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.

150→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
151→
152→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
153→
154→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
155→
156→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
157→
158→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
159→
160→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
161→
162→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
163→
164→```kotlin
165→public class SomeRule : Rule(
166→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
167→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
168→) {
169→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
170→
171→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
172→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
173→  }
174→  
175→  ...
176→}
177→```
178→
179→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
180→
181→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
182→
183→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
184→
185→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
186→
187→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
188→
189→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
190→
191→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
192→
193→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
194→
195→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
196→

CHANGELOG.md-482-
CHANGELOG.md-483-#### Rule lifecycle hooks / deprecate RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier
CHANGELOG.md-484-
CHANGELOG.md-485-Up until ktlint 0.46 the Rule class provided only one life cycle hook. This "visit" hook was called in a depth-first-approach on all nodes in the file. A rule like the IndentationRule used the RunOnRootOnly visitor modifier to call this lifecycle hook for the root node only in combination with an alternative way of traversing the ASTNodes. Downside of this approach was that suppression of the rule on blocks inside a file was not possible ([#631](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/631)). More generically, this applied to all rules, applying alternative traversals of the AST.

11→internal class ImportSorter(
12→    val patterns: List<PatternEntry>,
13→) : Comparator<KtImportDirective> {
14→    override fun compare(
15→        import1: KtImportDirective,
16→        import2: KtImportDirective,
17→    ): Int {
18→        val importPath1 = import1.importPath!!
19→        val importPath2 = import2.importPath!!
20→
21→        return compareValuesBy(
22→            importPath1,
23→            importPath2,
24→            { import -> findImportIndex(import) },
25→            { import -> import.toString().replace("`", "") },
26→        )
27→    }
28→
29→    fun findImportIndex(path: ImportPath): Int {
30→        var bestIndex: Int = -1
31→        var bestEntryMatch: PatternEntry? = null
32→        var allOtherAliasIndex = -1
33→        var allOtherIndex = -1
34→
35→        for ((index, entry) in patterns.withIndex()) {
36→            if (entry == PatternEntry.ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) {
37→                allOtherAliasIndex = index
38→            }
39→            if (entry == PatternEntry.ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) {
40→                allOtherIndex = index
41→            }
42→            if (entry.isBetterMatchForPackageThan(bestEntryMatch, path)) {
43→                bestEntryMatch = entry
44→                bestIndex = index
45→            }
46→        }
47→
48→        if (bestIndex == -1 && path.hasAlias() && allOtherAliasIndex == -1 && allOtherIndex != -1) {
49→            // if no layout for alias imports specified, put them among all others
50→            bestIndex = allOtherIndex
51→        }
52→        return bestIndex
53→    }

 10→public class PatternEntry(
 11→    packageName: String,
 12→    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13→    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14→) {
 15→    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16→
 17→    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19→        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20→
 21→        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22→            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23→                return true
 24→            }
 25→            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26→                return true
 27→            }
 28→        }
 29→        return false
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33→        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37→        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38→        import: ImportPath,
 39→    ): Boolean {
 40→        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41→        if (entry == null) return true
 42→
 43→        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44→        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46→        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47→
 48→        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49→
 50→        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51→    }
 52→

 54→        when (this) {
 55→            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56→            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57→            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
